steps.title: Track facts then uncertainty
steps.content: The passage first identifies the Federalist Papers, their authors, publication context, and purpose. It then shifts to an unresolved issue: the authorship of a few individual essays is uncertain.

steps.title: Reject inaccurate summaries
steps.content: Choice A misstates the authorship issue as one-person versus two-person essays. Choice B leaves out the uncertainty. Choice C invents disagreement among the authors about ratification. The structure is known background followed by something not fully known. The answer is D.
